summary: Attempted to replace the shell `replace_symbol`/`reverse_symbol` helpers with a Python `melee replace` CLI command that can batch-rename symbols across source and asm files. The PR also introduced a broader Python CLI package with `build` and `replace` subcommands, Black/editorconfig line-length settings, editable install via requirements.txt, and light cleanup to `parse_map.py`. It was closed unmerged after the author noted it would be implemented differently.
what_changed: Added a Python `melee replace` subcommand with alias `melee r`, accepting one or more `PATTERN=REPLACEMENT` pairs.
Removed the Bash `replace_symbol` and `reverse_symbol` functions from `tools/functions.sh`.
Implemented replacement options for word-boundary control (`--no-boundaries`), case-insensitive replacement (`--ignore-case`), and reversing pairs (`--reverse`).
Compiled all requested replacement patterns into one regular expression before walking files, rather than invoking one sed command per rename.
Scoped replacement traversal to `src/**/*.c` and `asm/**/*.s` via helper functions in `tools/cli/utils.py`.
Added a new Python CLI entry point package under `tools/cli` with `main.py`, `commands/build.py`, `commands/replace.py`, setuptools metadata, and a `melee = main:main` console script entry point.
Added `-e tools/cli` to `requirements.txt` so the CLI could be installed in editable mode.
Updated `tools/parse_map.py` formatting and exposed/used a `main()` function shape suitable for import from the new build command.
The new `build` subcommand wrapped common make workflow flags such as clean, rebuild, expected sync, map generation, non-matching, skip-check, jobs, format, logging, and dump generation.
smart_moves: Batching all requested symbol renames into a single compiled regex is a scalable approach for mass decomp renaming work.
Applying replacements from the end of the file toward the front avoids offset corruption while editing the same string repeatedly.
Keeping word boundaries on by default mirrors the original shell helper's intent and reduces accidental partial-symbol edits.
Providing `--reverse` directly in the CLI preserves the old `reverse_symbol` workflow without needing a separate function.
Using `argparse` subcommands and short aliases (`build`/`b`, `replace`/`r`) made the proposed tool ergonomic for repeated decomp workflows.
Centralizing repository paths in `tools/cli/utils.py` avoided hardcoding root/build/src/asm paths in every command.
decomp_lessons: Bulk symbol renaming tools should default to word-boundary-aware replacement; otherwise address-like names and prefixed symbols are easy to corrupt.
For many symbol renames, compile a single alternation regex from escaped patterns rather than repeatedly scanning the tree for each symbol.
When replacing multiple names in one pass, process match spans in reverse order if rewriting strings by slicing.
A reverse-renaming mode is useful when experimenting with names or backing out a batch rename.
Decomp tooling benefits from being importable as modules: this PR reshaped `parse_map.py` so build automation could call `parse_map.main()` after a map file is produced.
The PR was closed with the note that the implementation would be done differently, so future work should treat this as an exploratory design rather than accepted project direction.
Generated packaging artifacts such as `melee_cli.egg-info` were included in this PR; future tooling PRs should verify whether generated metadata belongs in source control before submitting.
naming_conventions: Replacement arguments used the explicit form `PATTERN=REPLACEMENT`.
CLI subcommands used full names with one-letter aliases: `replace`/`r` and `build`/`b`.
The example rename pattern converted generic address symbols like `func_80005BB0` to module-prefixed names like `lbColl_80005BB0`.
Python formatting was standardized toward Black with an 80-character line length.
Map CSV field names used camelCase keys such as `localAddress`, `globalAddress`, and `translationUnit`, preserving existing `parse_map.py` output naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The proposed rename command targeted both C source and assembly files, specifically `src/**/*.c` and `asm/**/*.s`.
The original shell helper had searched `asm` and `src` with sed word-boundary replacement; the Python port kept the same broad symbol-renaming purpose while adding batch operation.
The PR body's example shows the intended use case: quickly replacing multiple `func_XXXXXXXX` symbols with module-prefixed names in one command.
The build command could run make with `GENERATE_MAP=1`, parse `GALE01.map` into `build/map.csv`, and optionally call `dadosod` to dump `main.dol` using that map.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the slice.
The only issue comment says: "Will implement this in a different way."
Because the PR was closed unmerged, the approach should not be interpreted as accepted project tooling policy.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR or commit that implemented `replace_symbol` differently.
Search current tooling for `melee replace`, `replace_symbol`, or equivalent bulk rename commands.
Check whether generated `egg-info` files were later removed or avoided in CLI packaging work.
Compare this proposed CLI structure against PR #729, which this PR said it depended on.
